reasoning: The base rate anchor for the very near term (Apr 06) is 0.56 given historical upward trends for early April and last Friday's sudden drop which may partly revert. However, forecasts structurally project standard 30-year fixed rates to dip toward 6.25%-6.3% by late 2025 as the spread to 10-year Treasury normalizes or long rates drift slightly downward alongside Fed cuts. Since 6.318 is roughly the predicted baseline for end of 2025, the chances of exceeding 6.318 slowly drop over the horizon from slightly above 50% down to less than 50% (around 42% for March 2026), reflecting expected moderate easing in long-term yields and mortgage spreads.
